definitions: To shine intensely and oppressively, typically said of the sun.
To persuade someone to reduce the price of something; to bargain down.
To strike or hit repeatedly and forcefully, causing something to fall or break.
examples: The sun was beating down on us all afternoon.
She managed to beat down the seller by fifty dollars.
The heavy rain began to beat down the crops.
pastTenses: beat down (past simple), beaten down (past perfect)
